microsoft_ft: Scaled Azure's recovery validation program from a **2-person operation** into a platformized system, standardizing workflows to sustain 45+ annual resilience drills and drive **$14M+ business impact** across enterprise customers including Databricks, Walmart, SAP, and NetApp.

Led **0→1 development** of an internal Resilience Automation Platform, defining product requirements and self-service scheduling capabilities that reduced operational toil by 30% and transitioned execution to a fully scalable model.

Directed 14 cross-org recovery executions under executive visibility, including serving as bridge lead for a sovereign-cloud network isolation test tied to a **$1.5B+ enterprise contract**.
Built an internal **AI agent** and restructured planning workflows to reduce planning cycle time by 39% and increase drill capacity by 21%, scaling team leverage through intelligent automation.

Improved data retrieval performance by implementing rigorous metadata standards and migrating documentation to an **AI-powered semantic search** tool, cutting lookup time by 83% across engineering teams.

Engineered a **Power BI dashboard** tracking operational toil across 140+ teams, enabling leadership to target automation gaps and prioritize high-impact investments with data-driven precision.

pro_painters: Increased job bookings by **26%** by optimizing operations and managing the end-to-end scoping and invoicing lifecycle for 200+ monthly proposals through a redesigned CRM workflow.

tailoring_notes: 'AI-driven workflows' and 'AI agent' (microsoft_ft bullet 5, microsoft_2023 bullet 1) mirror the JD's 'Generative AI Experiences' and 'emerging animation technologies' framing, positioning Cyrus as someone who builds and ships AI-powered systems.

'Automated pipelines' and 'CI/CD pipelines' (amazon_robotics bullet 3, microsoft_2022 bullet 3) map to the JD's emphasis on 'animation pipelines and workflows' and technical collaboration with engineering teams.

'Self-service scheduling' and 'scalable model' (microsoft_ft bullet 2) echo the JD's 'real-time performance requirements' and 'ensure animations run efficiently across target platforms' language around systems that must operate reliably at scale.
Bullets tightened across all roles to eliminate 3-line overflow risk; each role held to its min count (5/3/3/3/3) rather than max to reclaim vertical space and fit one page.
